subject: Sports
title: Usain Bolt: The Fastest Human Ever
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Usain Bolt: The Fastest Human Ever.

hook.text: 9.58 seconds. The time it took Usain Bolt to run 100 meters in Berlin. Nobody has come close since.

buildup.text: Bolt was 6'5"—unusually tall for a sprinter. Coaches said his long legs would be a disadvantage at the start.

discovery.text: His stride length compensated for slower starts. He covered 100 meters in just 41 steps while rivals needed 44-45.

twist.text: Bolt slowed down and celebrated before the finish line in Beijing 2008—his 9.69 could have been even faster.

climax.text: He won eight Olympic golds and set records in 100m, 200m, and 4x100m that still stand today.

punchline.text: Too tall to sprint, they said. He became the fastest human in history.

quiz.question: What is Usain Bolt's 100m world record?
